summary: Ruth tells the story of a Moabite widow whose loyalty to Naomi leads to redemption through Boaz and places her in the genealogy of David.
historical_context: Set during the time of the judges, Ruth offers a counterpoint to national chaos by highlighting covenant loyalty, family restoration, and providence working through ordinary faithfulness.

mirror_water_fire.mirror: Loss reveals the vulnerability of human life and the need for covenant faithfulness.
mirror_water_fire.water: Acts of kindness and redemption cleanse bitterness with hope.
mirror_water_fire.fire: The story transforms grief into lineage, legacy, and royal purpose.
